Clinical Key Pointers:Sympathectomy is a surgical procedure that destroys nerves in the sympathetic nervous system.
The procedure increases blood flow and decreases long-term pain in certain diseases that cause
narrowed blood vessels. It can also be used to reduce excessive sweating. This surgical procedure
cuts or destroys the sympathetic ganglia, a collection of nerve cell bodies in clusters along the
thoracic or lumbar spinal cord.

[TABLE 1 | page 1]
| AB PM-JAY Package Name | AB PM-JAY Procedure Name | Procedure Code HBP 1.0. | Procedure Code HBP 2.1 | Procedure Code HBP 2022 | Package Price |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sympathectomy | Sympathectomy (U/L) | S100239 S100229 | SG073A | SG073A | NRP: Rs. 16,500/- Tier 3: Rs. 20,700/- Tier 2: Rs. 23,500/- Tier 1: Rs. 24,800/- |
| Sympathectomy | Sympathectomy (B/L) | S100239 S100229 | - | SG073B | NRP: Rs. 35,000/- Tier 3: Rs. 35,000/- Tier 2: Rs. 41,000/- Tier 1: Rs. 43,800/- |

o
Sympathectomy is performed to relieve ischemia when the fingers, toes, ears, or
nose are exposed to cold (Raynaud's phenomenon)
o
Sympathectomy may help treat Reflex Sympathetic Dystrophy (RSD), which
sometimes develops after an injury.
o
Sympathectomy also effectively treats excessive sweating (Hyperhidrosis) of the
palms, armpits, or face.
o
Rest pain in Thromboangitis obliterans (TAO)
*

Cervical Sympathectomy▪
In this operation, the sympathetic trunk from the lower half of the stellate
ganglion to just below the 3rd thoracic ganglion is removed.
